title: Lab 39: SSH Key Authentication and Rsync
description: Configure key-based SSH access and securely transfer files between systems

tasks: Ensure the user mesh39 exists on both clientvm and servervm, and set the password on both systems to cinder9.
As user mesh39 on clientvm, generate an ED25519 SSH key pair with no passphrase.
Configure passwordless SSH access for mesh39 from clientvm to servervm using public key authentication.
Using rsync over SSH, copy the directory /home/mesh39/client-data/ from clientvm to /home/mesh39/server-data/ on servervm.
hints: Use the mesh39 account for the SSH key work.
Verify the transfer by checking the copied file on servervm through SSH.
checks: getent passwd mesh39 >/dev/null && getent shadow mesh39 | awk -F: 'length($2)>0 && $2 !~ /^(!!?|\*|LK|NP)$/' >/dev/null
ssh admin@servervm sudo getent passwd mesh39 >/dev/null && ssh admin@servervm sudo getent shadow mesh39 | awk -F: 'length($2)>0 && $2 !~ /^(!!?|\*|LK|NP)$/' >/dev/null
runuser -l mesh39 -c 'ssh -o BatchMode=yes -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no mesh39@192.168.122.3 true'
solution_outline: Use ssh-keygen, authorized_keys, and rsync over SSH.
task_titles: Ensure mesh39 exists on both systems
generate an ED25519 SSH key pair with no passphrase
Configure passwordless SSH access for mesh39 from...
Using rsync over SSH, copy the directory...
